name: Croque Madame Sandwich
instructions: 1. Set an oven rack about a few inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. 2. In a saucepan over medium-low heat, melt 1 1/of butter. Whisk in flour and stir until slightly brown, about 3 minutes. Whisk in milk; bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Once boiling, reduce heat immediately and simmer for 5 minutes. 3. Add of Gruyère cheese, salt, pepper, and nutmeg; heat until cheese is melted, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside. 4. Spread about of butter on 2 pieces of bread. Spread of cheese sauce on top and layer each with a ham slice. 5. Spread the butter on the remaining 2 pieces of bread, then spread Dijon mustard on top and sprinkle with remaining Gruyère. 6. Transfer all 4 pieces to a baking sheet. Broil until cheese is almost melted, 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from the oven, place toast slices together to make 2 sandwiches, and pour remaining cheese sauce on top of each sandwich. Return to the broiler until sauce starts to brown, about 2 minutes. 7. While sandwiches are broiling, heat remaining butter in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Crack 2 eggs into the skillet, keeping them separate. Season with the salt and pepper. Add the water, cover, and baste until whites are set but yolks are still runny, about 2 minutes. 8. Top each sandwich with an egg and sprinkle with the dill, if using. Serve immediately.
simpleInstructions: 1. Preheat the oven's broiler. 2. Make cheese sauce: Melt the butter, stir in the flour and brown. Add the milk, bring to boil, simmer. Add the cheese, salt, pepper, nutmeg. 3. Butter and prepare bread slices with the cheese sauce, ham, Dijon mustard, and Gruyère. 4. Broil until cheese is melted. Assemble sandwiches, pour sauce, and broil again. 5. Fry eggs, top sandwiches with the eggs and dill. Serve.
